title: task
description: rust/lichen-ipv6/src/lib.rs:459-471 - The build() method uses 'let _ = pkt.push(...)' and 'let _ = pkt.extend_from_slice(data)' which silently ignores capacity errors from the heapless Vec<u8, 128>. If echo data exceeds 120 bytes (128 minus 8-byte header), data is silently truncated. The checksum is then computed over incomplete data, producing a malformed packet. Fix: return Result and propagate errors, or cap input data size with explicit check.
close_reason: Fixed: Changed handle_icmpv6 return type from Option to Result<Option, Ipv6Error> and replaced .ok()? with ? to properly propagate parsing and buffer errors instead of silently returning None
